entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A cereal crop used for stovers silage

entities.name: SILAGE
entities.description: Fermented high-moisture stored fodder

entities.name: FERMENTATION
entities.description: The chemical breakdown of a substance by bacteria, fungi, and other microorganisms

entities.name: PH
entities.description: A measure of acidity or alkalinity used to evaluate fermentation progress

entities.name: COMPLETELY RANDOMIZED EXPERIMENTAL DESIGN
entities.description: An experimental design where treatments are assigned to experimental units completely at random

entities.name: FACTORIAL SCHEME
entities.description: An experimental design that allows for the study of multiple factors simultaneously

entities.name: CULTIVARS
entities.description: Planted varieties of a crop

entities.name: CUTTING STAGES
entities.description: The growth stage at which the crop is harvested

entities.name: SALT ADDITION
entities.description: The addition of salt to the silage to influence fermentation

entities.name: ROOM TEMPERATURE
entities.description: The ambient temperature at which silages were stored

entities.name: SADORE
entities.description: A local cultivar of pearl millet in Niger

entities.name: SIAKA MILLET
entities.description: A local cultivar of pearl millet in Niger

entities.name: NIGER
entities.description: The country where the local millet cultivars are sourced

entities.name: IN VITRO ORGANIC MATTER DIGESTIBILITY
entities.description: A measure of the digestibility of organic matter in a laboratory setting

entities.name: DRY MATTER CONTENT BEFORE SILAGE
entities.description: The amount of dry matter in stovers prior to the ensiling process

entities.name: DRY MATTER CONTENT OF SILAGES
entities.description: The amount of dry matter remaining in the silage

entities.name: NEUTRAL DETERGENT FIBER
entities.description: A measure of the total cell wall constituents of forage

entities.name: ACID DETERGENT FIBER
entities.description: A measure of cellulose and lignin in forage

entities.name: ACID DETERGENT LIGNIN
entities.description: A measure of the lignin content in forage

entities.name: CRUDE PROTEIN
entities.description: The total protein content of the silage

entities.name: METABOLIZABLE ENERGY
entities.description: The energy available to the animal after digestion

entities.name: ASH CONTENT
entities.description: The inorganic residue remaining after the combustion of organic matter

entities.name: ANIMAL FEED
entities.description: Food specifically formulated for livestock

relationships.description: Stovers of pearl millet are used to produce silage
Silage is produced through a fermentation process
pH evolution is used to evaluate the progress of fermentation
Used to evaluate factors affecting pearl millet silage
Used to evaluate factors affecting pearl millet silage
Different cultivars show variation in silage pH evolution
Cutting stages are a factor evaluated in silage quality
Salt addition is a factor evaluated in silage quality
Sadoré is a specific cultivar of pearl millet
Siaka is a specific cultivar of pearl millet
Sadoré is a local cultivar found in Niger
Siaka is a local cultivar found in Niger
Increasing pH values are associated with increasing dry matter content before silage
Increasing pH values are associated with increasing dry matter content of silages
Increasing pH values are associated with increasing neutral detergent fiber
Increasing pH values are associated with increasing acid detergent fiber
Increasing pH values are associated with increasing acid detergent lignin
Increasing pH values are associated with decreasing crude protein
Increasing pH values are associated with decreasing metabolizable energy
Increasing pH values are associated with decreasing in vitro organic matter digestibility
Increasing pH values are associated with decreasing ash values
Millet stover silage is suitable for use as animal feed
